name: icon
title: Icon
description: A dependency-free SVG icon primitive with a curated 23-glyph starter set drawn on a shared 16x16 grid. Standardizes the paint contract (currentColor stroke, forced-colors safe), sizing (1em by default so icons scale with adjacent text, plus named sizes and arbitrary), RTL mirroring for directional glyphs, and the accessibility contract: decorative by default (aria-hidden, focusable=false) or meaningful (role=img with an accessible name) when a label or title is provided.
useWhen: Placing a small glyph beside text (a button label, list item, menu entry)
Needing a standalone meaningful icon with an accessible name and no visible text
Standardizing icon size, color inheritance, and RTL flipping across a design system without pulling in an icon library
avoidWhen: You need a large illustration or spot art rather than a UI glyph
The glyph must carry status meaning by color alone — pair it with text or a label instead
A logo or brand mark is required (icons here are single-color, currentColor-stroked UI glyphs)

keyboard: Icons are not interactive and add no tab stops
SVGs are marked focusable=false so they never become a stray tab stop in browsers that focus SVGs
Wrap an icon in a Button or Link when it needs to be operable
accessibility.targetSize: Not applicable — Icon is presentational; the interactive control that wraps it owns the 44px target
notes: Decorative by default: with no label or title the icon is aria-hidden and focusable=false, so a screen reader skips it and it is not announced twice beside a visible text label.
Meaningful when a label or title is provided: the icon gets role=img and that accessible name, for the rare standalone icon that is the only thing conveying meaning.
currentColor stroke (never a hardcoded fill) means the icon inherits text color and stays visible in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode, where a background-image icon would disappear.
Directional glyphs (chevrons, arrows, external-link) carry rtl:-scale-x-100 so they point along the reading direction in RTL; the flip prop overrides per instance.
